description: §61.3 — an `<endpoint>` whose `accepts=` names an UNDECLARED type (`NopeEnum`) fires E-TYPE-UNKNOWN-NAME, reusing the §14.1.2 unknown-name machinery (no dedicated E-ENDPOINT-* code). An undeclared ref does NOT also fire E-ENDPOINT-ACCEPTS-NOT-ENUM (no double-report — the type never resolved to a concrete non-enum).
rationale: §61.9: an undeclared `accepts=` ref reuses E-TYPE-UNKNOWN-NAME (the §14.1.2 machinery, exactly as `<api>`'s reqShape/responseType). E-ENDPOINT-ACCEPTS-NOT-ENUM fires only when the type RESOLVES to a concrete non-enum, so it stays silent here.
